titleStmt.title: Letter from Major Lennon to Father Aloysius 2 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Major Lennon

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Refers to the execution of P.H. Pearse. Major Lennon grants Fr Aloysius permission to visit. Fr. Aloysius was a Capuchin priest. Includes Fr. Aloysius's response. Includes detail that Thomas MacDonagh also required his services (the last rites). Executions took place at 3.30 am on 3rd May 1916.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Kilmainham Detention Barracks 2-5-16 Sir The prisoner H.T. Pearse desires to see you &
you have permission to visit him. He Failing
you he would be glad to see any of the
Capucinis. I am Sir,
Your obedient servant
W.S. Lennon.
Major
Commandant.
Rev. F Aloysius.
This was the summons to attend the late
P.H. Pearse. I received the notice about
10 pm on 2nd May. On arrival at Kilmainham
I was notified that Thomas MacDonagh
also desired my services. The executions
took place about 3.30 a.m. on 3rd May.
Rev. Fr Aloysius O.S.F.C.
